scope: Open access Nature Portfolio journal for high-quality research relevant to digital medicine and health, including clinical application and implementation of digital/mobile technologies, virtual healthcare, validated AI and machine learning models, clinical informatics, digital biomarkers, digital tools, ethics, policy, regulation, security, and digital transformation.

formatting.abstract_style: Unstructured abstract; no subheadings for Articles
formatting.reference_style: Nature Portfolio style; verify in current author guidance
formatting.figure_table_policy: Multi-panel figures should be on a single page with panels labeled a), b), c); figure legends are limited to 350 words per figure.
special_requirements: Article abstract up to 150 words with no subheadings.
Clinical trials must include registration number and registration date.
Randomized clinical trials require CONSORT or appropriate extension checklist.
Ethics approval and consent details are mandatory for human/animal participants, materials, or data.

indexing.notes: Journal information lists PubMedCentral, SCOPUS, and Science Citation Index Expanded; PubMed/MEDLINE status should be re-verified before displaying production badges.

business_model.license: Creative Commons Attribution licenses; verify exact license at submission.

fit_strengths: Strong fit for clinically validated digital medicine, AI, wearable, sensor, virtual care, and digital biomarker studies.
Strong fit when the manuscript shows clinical application, implementation, interoperability, or transformation of healthcare.
Strong fit for high-quality digital health research with clear ethics, governance, data, and code transparency.
fit_risks: Risk increases for off-the-shelf AI or digital tool studies without novelty or validation.
Risk increases for purely observational, case-study, preclinical, or small preliminary work.
Risk increases if clinical trial registration, ethics, consent, data availability, or code availability requirements are missing.

checklist: Use an unstructured Article abstract up to 150 words.
Use Introduction, Results, Discussion, Methods ordering for Articles.
Add mandatory data availability, author contributions, and competing interests statements.
Include trial registration details for clinical trials.
Include CONSORT or appropriate checklist for randomized clinical trials.
Avoid recommending for small preliminary or purely observational digital-tool studies.

evidence_sources.label: npj Digital Medicine aims and scope
